hypothesis: In chemical systems with three or more metastable states forming branching reaction networks, composing conditional scores from all relevant states via multi-way isodensity interpolation and applying Score-Aligned Ascent will systematically identify distinct first-order saddle points for each branching pathway—including intermediate saddle points between nonadjacent minima not seen during training—producing sampled configurations whose committor to the two adjacent minima is centered at 0.5 within ±0.1.
